source.book: 《9分达人雅思听力真题还原及解析7》

segments.text: Peter: Hi, Shona.
segments.translation: 嗨，肖娜。

segments.text: Shona: Hi, Peter.
segments.translation: 嗨，彼得。

segments.text: Shona: Have you made up your mind about which internship programme to apply for?
segments.translation: 你决定申请哪个实习项目了吗？

segments.text: Peter: Yes, pretty much.
segments.translation: 嗯，差不多决定了。

segments.text: Shona: I'm entirely at a loss.
segments.translation: 我完全不知道该怎么选。

segments.text: Shona: I mean, there are so many good ones.
segments.translation: 我的意思是，优秀的项目太多了。

segments.text: Shona: Can you give me some tips?
segments.translation: 你能给我一些建议吗？

segments.text: Peter: Sure.
segments.translation: 当然。

segments.text: Shona: What is your top priority when weighing up these programmes?
segments.translation: 衡量这些项目时，你最优先考虑的是什么？

segments.text: Peter: Well, I guess I'd go for ones that can help me with the skill set I lack for my future career.
segments.translation: 嗯，我想我会选择那些能帮助我补足未来职业所需技能的项目。

segments.text: Peter: I still need a lot of practical training in dealing with statistics.
segments.translation: 在处理统计数据方面，我仍然需要大量实践训练。

segments.text: Peter: But keep in mind that you don't want to set your goal too grand, like drawing up a business plan.
segments.translation: 但要记住，目标不要定得太宏大，比如制订一份商业计划。

segments.text: Peter: You might end up nowhere.
segments.translation: 否则你最后可能一无所获。

segments.text: Shona: I see.
segments.translation: 我明白了。

segments.text: Shona: So, I want to learn how to organise ideas and present them.
segments.translation: 那么，我想学习如何组织观点并将其表达出来。

segments.text: Peter: Good.
segments.translation: 很好。

segments.text: Peter: Now you've nailed step one.
segments.translation: 现在你已经完成第一步了。

segments.text: Shona: Now what?
segments.translation: 接下来呢？

segments.text: Peter: You must also consider your strengths for a suitable internship programme.
segments.translation: 你还必须考虑自己的优势，以选择合适的实习项目。

segments.text: Peter: Otherwise, you might get rejected or end up with one that you are incapable of doing.
segments.translation: 否则，你可能会遭到拒绝，或者最终得到一份自己无力胜任的实习。

segments.text: Shona: But I have no idea what I'm good at.
segments.translation: 但我不知道自己擅长什么。

segments.text: Peter: Communicate with people around you or whom you've worked with.
segments.translation: 和你身边的人或曾与你共事的人聊一聊。

segments.text: Peter: That's what I did.
segments.translation: 我当时就是这么做的。

segments.text: Peter: I thought I was a good team leader, but many people I've worked with for various projects mentioned something else.
segments.translation: 我原以为自己是个优秀的团队负责人，但许多在不同项目中与我共事过的人提到了另一项优点。

segments.text: Peter: They admire that I could work around the clock to finish projects on time.
segments.translation: 他们很佩服我能夜以继日地工作，按时完成项目。

segments.text: Peter: And indeed, I'm more focused and perform better when I'm pushed for time.
segments.translation: 事实上，时间紧迫时我会更加专注，表现也更好。

segments.text: Shona: Good point.
segments.translation: 说得有道理。

segments.text: Shona: So what do you plan to do next?
segments.translation: 那你下一步打算做什么？

segments.text: Peter: I'll visit the careers officer to help me with the school application form.
segments.translation: 我会去找就业指导老师，请他帮我处理学校的申请表。

segments.text: Peter: You know, the one we have to submit before applying to companies.
segments.translation: 你知道，就是那份申请公司前必须提交的表格。

segments.text: Shona: But it is unnecessary to fill it in.
segments.translation: 但那份表没必要填写。

segments.text: Shona: It's only optional.
segments.translation: 它只是选填的。

segments.text: Peter: Really?
segments.translation: 真的吗？

segments.text: Peter: I'm glad I heard from you.
segments.translation: 幸好听你说了。

segments.text: Peter: Then I'll ask the careers officer to look at my personal statement and give me some tips on polishing it up.
segments.translation: 那我会请就业指导老师看看我的个人陈述，并给我一些润色建议。

segments.text: Shona: Don't you need help with your CV?
segments.translation: 你的简历不需要别人帮忙吗？

segments.text: Peter: No, I'm good.
segments.translation: 不用，我能处理。

segments.text: Peter: I have already learned to write it in class.
segments.translation: 我已经在课堂上学过怎么写了。

segments.text: Shona: That's good.
segments.translation: 那很好。

segments.text: Shona: So what kind of company did you choose?
segments.translation: 那么你选择了哪类公司？

segments.text: Peter: I've decided to apply for an IT company.
segments.translation: 我决定申请一家信息技术公司。

segments.text: Shona: Why is that?
segments.translation: 为什么呢？

segments.text: Peter: Well, even though I've learned how the IT industry developed in the past from different lectures, I know nothing about its latest trends.
segments.translation: 嗯，虽然我从不同的讲座中了解了信息技术行业过去的发展历程，但对它的最新趋势一无所知。

segments.text: Peter: I'm prepared to work in an IT company and fill in the information gap.
segments.translation: 我准备到一家信息技术公司工作，填补这方面的信息空白。

segments.text: Shona: I see.
segments.translation: 我明白了。

segments.text: Shona: What do you plan to do next term?
segments.translation: 你下学期打算做什么？

segments.text: Peter: I think I'll stick to most of the optional modules I've chosen.
segments.translation: 我想我会继续修读自己选择的大部分选修模块。

segments.text: Peter: But regarding the language sessions, I'm doing poorly in Japanese.
segments.translation: 但说到语言课程，我的日语学得很差。

segments.text: Peter: I'd better drop it before wasting more time.
segments.translation: 我最好退掉这门课，免得浪费更多时间。

segments.text: Peter: I think I'll learn Spanish instead.
segments.translation: 我想改学西班牙语。

segments.text: Shona: Oh, I learned Spanish for a year.
segments.translation: 哦，我学过一年西班牙语。

segments.text: Shona: I think you'll find it interesting.
segments.translation: 我想你会觉得它很有意思。

segments.text: Shona: Do you know what job opportunities these companies offer interns?
segments.translation: 你知道这些公司为实习生提供哪些工作机会吗？

segments.text: Peter: I've searched online and browsed some of the schemes.
segments.translation: 我在网上搜索过，也浏览了其中一些实习方案。

segments.text: Peter: Do you know SAIC?
segments.translation: 你知道SAIC吗？

segments.text: Shona: Yes.
segments.translation: 知道。

segments.text: Shona: I heard they offer internship positions dealing with customer interaction management.
segments.translation: 我听说他们提供负责客户互动管理的实习岗位。

segments.text: Shona: But I'm not quite sure what that means.
segments.translation: 但我不太确定那具体是做什么。

segments.text: Peter: Well, the position offers the chance to become fully aware of customer value and keep in touch with customers to keep them highly engaged and satisfied.
segments.translation: 这个岗位让你有机会充分了解客户价值，并与客户保持联系，使他们始终保持高度参与和满意。

segments.text: Peter: These include activities such as PR, communications, marketing and customer service.
segments.translation: 工作内容包括公共关系、沟通、市场营销和客户服务等活动。

segments.text: Shona: Sounds like loads of work to do.
segments.translation: 听起来工作量很大。

segments.text: Shona: What about Gemini Technologies?
segments.translation: Gemini Technologies怎么样？

segments.text: Peter: It's an IT consulting company.
segments.translation: 那是一家信息技术咨询公司。

segments.text: Peter: Although many employees say it's stressful working there, I want to apply for this one.
segments.translation: 虽然许多员工说在那里工作压力很大，但我想申请这家公司。

segments.text: Shona: Why is that?
segments.translation: 为什么呢？

segments.text: Peter: Actually, the company provides service to improve software architecture.
segments.translation: 实际上，这家公司提供改善软件架构的服务。

segments.text: Peter: But that's pretty demanding as a team of software engineers has to provide detailed analysis and suggestions for required changes before the deadline.
segments.translation: 但这项工作要求很高，因为软件工程师团队必须在截止日期前提供详细分析和所需变更的建议。

segments.text: Peter: As an observer and participant of a particular project, an intern can learn how to work to a strict deadline.
segments.translation: 作为某个具体项目的观察者和参与者，实习生可以学会如何在严格的截止期限内完成工作。

segments.text: Shona: That's not my area of expertise.
segments.translation: 那不是我的专长领域。

segments.text: Shona: But I'm sure you'll excel at this.
segments.translation: 但我相信你会做得很出色。

segments.text: Peter: I certainly do hope so.
segments.translation: 我当然也希望如此。

segments.text: Peter: Have you checked out Deloitte?
segments.translation: 你了解过德勤吗？

segments.text: Shona: Of course.
segments.translation: 当然。

segments.text: Shona: That's a renowned accounting firm, and thus its internships offer excellent learning experiences in this regard.
segments.translation: 那是一家知名会计师事务所，因此它的实习项目能在这方面提供极佳的学习经历。

segments.text: Shona: You know, things like examining a company's financial statements - the income statement, balance sheet and cash flow statement.
segments.translation: 你知道，就是审查公司的财务报表，比如利润表、资产负债表和现金流量表。

segments.text: Shona: I want to give it a try.
segments.translation: 我想试一试。

segments.text: Shona: But I might get rejected, so I need a backup plan.
segments.translation: 但我可能会被拒绝，所以需要一个备用计划。

segments.text: Shona: Have you read anything about Kearney?
segments.translation: 你看过有关科尔尼的资料吗？

segments.text: Peter: Yes.
segments.translation: 看过。

segments.text: Peter: It is another consulting firm.
segments.translation: 那是另一家咨询公司。

segments.text: Peter: It provides opportunities to reach out to other business partners and pay attention to networking.
segments.translation: 它提供了与其他商业伙伴接洽并注重拓展人脉的机会。

segments.text: Shona: Well, that sounds interesting.
segments.translation: 嗯，听起来很有意思。

segments.text: Peter: I'm thinking about Vortex.
segments.translation: 我在考虑Vortex。

segments.text: Peter: But I haven't got anything useful from its website.
segments.translation: 但我没从它的网站上找到任何有用的信息。

segments.text: Shona: I worked there last summer.
segments.translation: 我去年夏天在那里工作过。

segments.text: Shona: It's an international company with headquarters in Spain, so most of the managers here speak Spanish.
segments.translation: 那是一家总部位于西班牙的国际公司，所以这里的大多数经理都会说西班牙语。

segments.text: Shona: Thanks to the internship experience, I became fluent in it.
segments.translation: 多亏了那段实习经历，我的西班牙语变得很流利。

segments.text: Peter: Right.
segments.translation: 好吧。

segments.text: Peter: That's not for me.
segments.translation: 那不适合我。
